steps.title: Translate the minimum and maximum
steps.content: The minimum recorded height is 44 feet, so no tree can be shorter than 44 feet: $t \geq 44$. The maximum is 61 feet, so no tree can be taller than 61 feet: $t \leq 61$.

steps.title: Combine the bounds
steps.content: Both constraints must hold simultaneously for every recorded value, giving the compound inequality $44 \leq t \leq 61$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
Evaluate each choice:
  • $t \leq 44$: Fails for any tree taller than 44 feet (e.g., $t = 52$). Eliminated.
  • $t \geq 61$: Fails for any tree shorter than 61 feet (e.g., $t = 44$). Eliminated.
  • $t \geq 105$: No recorded height reaches 105 feet. Fails for all values. Eliminated.
  • $44 \leq t \leq 61$: True for every value in the recorded range. That matches Choice D — $44 \leq t \leq 61$.
